headlines.title: Deal between U.S., Iran has been reached says Pakistan's prime minister
headlines.description: Prime Minister of Pakistan Shehbaz Sharif announced that a deal between the U.S. and Iran has been reached with a signing expected on Friday in Switzerland. NBC News' Keir Simmons, Gabe Gutierrez and Matt Bradley report.

headlines.title: Trump, bald eagles and Zac Brown Band: Inside the UFC Freedom 250 Fan Fest
headlines.description: Ahead of the UFC fight card on the White House lawn, thousands descended on Washington D.C. to celebrate the sport, country and President Trump.

headlines.title: Despite war, inflation and sinking polling, Trump takes comfort in giving D.C. a face-lift
headlines.description: Facing one of the roughest patches of his second term, Trump is devoting outsize energy to giving Washington its biggest facelift in living memory.

headlines.title: Morocco’s draw against Brazil showed its potential to be the World Cup’s ultimate dark horse
headlines.description: Four years after becoming the first Arab team to reach the World Cup semifinals, Morocco arrives with a roster of European stars — including Real Madrid’s Brahim Diaz and captain Achraf Hakimi, fresh of a Champions League title with Paris-St. Germain.

headlines.title: Curaçao celebrates historic first World Cup goal before Germany rolls to 7-1 victory
headlines.description: Kai Havertz scored two goals as Germany pulled away from World Cup first-timer Curaçao late in the opening half and piled on after that in a 7-1 win Sunday in a group match

headlines.title: Coaching the U.S. was a big test for Mauricio Pochettino. Then his World Cup opener went ‘amazing.’
headlines.description: Pochettino, a 54-year-old Argentine, has been on the job for 20 months, but Friday’s game was unquestionably his biggest opportunity to make an impression on a wider fanbase tuning in to watch the U.S. for the first time.

headlines.title: Folarin Balogun chose the U.S. over England. It’s already paying off in the World Cup.
headlines.description: Folarin Balogun, born in Brooklyn but raised in London, agreed to play his international career with the Americans in 2023. Three years later he opened the World Cup with two goals.

headlines.title: Gol de Daichi Kamada y vuelve el empate para Japón tras un doble cabezazo
headlines.description: Japón aprovechó un tiro de esquina para fabricar una jugada de doble cabezazo dentro del área. Daichi Kamada apareció en el momento justo para rematar al fondo de la red y devolverle el empate a los Samurai Blue ante Países Bajos.

headlines.title: Ecuador quiere demostrar que es el “Caballo Negro”, y llega a Philadelphia
headlines.description: La Selección de Ecuador se hace presente en Pennsylvania, para afrontar a la competitiva Selección de Costa de Marfil.

headlines.title: Nathaniel Brown: “Es un sentimiento indescriptible”
headlines.description: El zaguero alemán, que marcó un gol en su primer partido mundialista, señaló que deben trabajar en sus debilidades

headlines.title: Félix Torres: "Nos preparamos bien, trabajamos para el debut"
headlines.description: El jugador ecuatoriano destacó la preparación y motivación con la que su selección afronta el debut ante Costa de Marfil, y la importancia de llevarse los tres puntos para empezar a destacar en su grupo, frente a la clasificación.

headlines.title: Gol de Crysencio Summerville devuelve la ventaja a Países Bajos ante Japón
headlines.description: Ryan Gravenberch volvió a aparecer como asistente al habilitar a Crysencio Summerville, quien definió de zurda dentro del área para devolverle la ventaja a Países Bajos frente a Japón.
